definition:
ἀντί-λημψις  (Rec. -ληψις; see MM, VGT see word; M, Pr., 56), -εως, ἡ 
 (ἀντιλαμβάνομαι) [in LXX for עֹז, זְרוֹעַ, etc., frequently in Psa.2:1-12,3Mac.; frequently also in π. in petitions to the Ptolemies in sense of βοήθεια (see Deiss., LAE, 107; BS, 92, 223) ;] 
  1. cl. a laying hold of, an exchange. 
   2. Hellenistic (LXX, π.) help: pl. of ministrations of deacons; 1Co.12:28 (DB, ii, 347 f.; Cremer, 386).†
 (AS)
